category_title: At-risk high school students in online credit recovery with prior self-discipline and engagement challenges
minimal_context: The student is taking pretests and working through modules in an online summer credit recovery course to achieve mastery.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start the pretest for the next module. You need to pass it to move forward in your summer credit recovery course.

STUDENT: I tried the pretest. I just think I concentrate better when it's just me and the work, but I still failed it.

TUTOR: You need to review the module and do the homework assignments.

STUDENT: I need the ability to keep doing the homework until I understand why I got the problems wrong. Right now I'm just getting stressed.

TUTOR: Just keep reading the text and try your best on the assignments.

STUDENT: I'm not doing the homework. Well the number one reason I didn't pass the course is because I was a lazy kid, and I'm just not going to study if I don't understand it.

options.A: Respond by saying, "Another module already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.B: Immediately remove all course demands and module materials from the student for 60 seconds.
options.C: Tell the student that his decision to not do the homework is a good idea.
options.D: Provide module content and homework assignments that address the student's current level of understanding.
